data.narrative:
Kepler Communications is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $233M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-market companies in the same sector and stage, with particular focus on 5 direct comparables: Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method, which applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $233M capital raised proxy, implies a valuation of $396M. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of the five same-stage, same-sector comparables at $1.5B, with the peer range spanning $1.0B to $2.5B.

The triangulated valuation range is $337M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $948M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-normalization versus peer-comparable positioning. The peer-median approach ($1.5B) appears more reflective of market precedent given the depth of comparable transactions, while the stage-progression method ($396M) may underweight sector-specific momentum. Revenue data is unavailable, precluding public-comp P/S anchoring (sector median 45.3x). Resolution requires either disclosed financials or clarity on Kepler's competitive positioning within the peer set.
